title: Homalium decurrens
intro: Homalium decurrens is a plant species belonging to the family Salicaceae, commonly known as the willow family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Homalium decurrens include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In herbal medicine, Homalium decurrens is typically prepared as a tea or decoction, and it is also used in traditional remedies, such as infusions and ointments, to promote wound healing and alleviate pain.
